assumption_text: The exposed civilian footprint is the encircled Budapest urban area after 26 December 1944, not Hungary as a whole and not the wider Budapest offensive front.
rationale: Sources explicitly describe trapped Budapest civilians and city fighting; national denominators would be grossly misleading.

assumption_text: Because Budapest inhabitants were not evacuated, average exposed civilians are bounded close to the 800,000 trapped-population claim (740k-800k average across the episode).
rationale: Ungváry explicitly says the inhabitants were never evacuated; attrition within the siege reduces average exposure only modestly relative to the starting total.

assumption_text: Average exposed military strength is lower than the 256,000 combined start strengths because the encircled defenders attrited sharply and daily attacker strength inside the urban fight likely varied.
rationale: No daily force-strength series was recovered in the quick-access pass, so person-months must be bounded from start strengths and casualty intensity.

assumption_text: Arrow Cross mass executions of Budapest Jews are counted as direct civilian deaths in the core DR estimate.
rationale: They are explicit violent killings during the relevant urban episode, and the siege source itself states that 15,000 of the civilian dead were killed in such mass executions.

assumption_text: Unknown-status direct deaths are left unallocated rather than proportionally assigned to civilians or military.
rationale: The source set did not recover a defensible unknown-status series, and silent allocation would create false precision.

assumption_text: Episode duration is treated as 50 days (26 December 1944 through 13 February 1945) = 1.64 months for person-month calculations.
rationale: This matches the standard siege window used in accessible Budapest battle summaries.
